displayTitle: Flaw, Flux, Technique: The Decision Chain

beats.narration: Three lessons collapse into one working question: which way must the flux run, and what equipment makes it run that way? Pure practice now.

payload.title: THE DECISION CHAIN
payload.sub: Which way must the flux run — and what makes it run that way?

beats.narration: Run the chain. Where do cracks run? Point flux across them, forty-five to ninety degrees. Pick the technique that builds it. Choose current for depth.
One shaft, two shots. The head shot's circular field catches axial seams. The fillet's circumferential crack needs flux along the axis, so add the coil.
Choosing the field is half the job. Proving it is the rest: pie gauge for direction, QQI shim for adequacy, gaussmeter for measured gauss.

payload.heading: Prove the field before you inspect

beats.narration: Flaw, flux, technique, current. Prove it, then inspect. Train the chain until it is reflex.

payload.sub: Train the chain until it's reflex.
